utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality critical summaries that passed all verifier checks and satisfied most judge evaluation criteria. The wi_skills run (10 steps) explicitly invoked the academic-researcher skill, which provided a structured five-dimension analysis framework, resulting in clearly labeled sections ('Why This Method Makes Sense', 'What The Numbers Mean', 'Methodological Concerns', 'Discussion Questions') that make the critical framework more transparent and reproducible. The wo_skills run (6 steps) also produced substantive, rigorous summaries with genuine methodological critique, effect size interpretation, and gap identification — performing surprisingly well without the skill scaffold. Both runs failed J1.4 equally due to the same umlaut omission ('Kühn' → 'Kuhn') and minor APA italics formatting inconsistency. Overall, wi_skills is marginally more effective: the skill-guided structure is more explicit, section headings are more pedagogically useful for seminar anchoring, and the coverage of all five analytical dimensions is more systematic. However, the quality gap between the two runs is smaller than the task description anticipated, as the wo_skills agent independently produced rigorous critical content.

utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality, formally structured research proposals that satisfy all six evaluation criteria. Both identified a specific literature gap, sharpened the research question, proposed an appropriate longitudinal design, integrated SDT as a theoretical framework, followed a logical argumentative arc, and correctly differentiated citation formats across source types. The wo_skills output is actually larger (18,912 bytes vs. 11,976 bytes) and uses a three-wave design compared to the wi_skills two-wave design, suggesting slightly greater methodological detail. However, the wi_skills output was produced more efficiently (3 steps vs. 8 steps) via the /academic-researcher skill invocation. In terms of substantive quality across all evaluation dimensions, the two proposals are effectively equivalent — both would meet fellowship committee standards. The wi_skills run demonstrates the skill's efficiency advantage, while wo_skills shows the baseline agent is also capable of producing a rigorous proposal when given sufficient context. Overall quality is similar, with wi_skills having a process efficiency advantage.
